summary: binding.gyp abuses GYP command-expansion to execute index.js at npm install
details: The package ships a binding.gyp whose sources array contains a GYP command-expansion entry (`<!(node index.js...)`), with target type `none` and no native C/C++ source files. When npm installs a package that ships a binding.gyp, it automatically invokes `node-gyp rebuild`, and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step. This runs `node index.js` on the installer's machine at install time without any declared preinstall/install/postinstall lifecycle script — a lifecycle-hook smuggling technique. The executed index.js reads host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, GITHUB_WORKSPACE) and writes them to `PWNED_BY_GYP.txt` in the workspace, staging identifier data in a location that subsequent CI steps could read. The absence of any real native sources confirms the binding.gyp exists solely to execute the embedded command, not to build a native addon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
